explanationZhTw: 這裡是刻意的反常說法。一般時鐘不會敲到十三點，用來營造不自然、壓抑的氛圍。
generatedExample: The town square clock was striking thirteen, which made everyone feel uneasy.

explanationZhTw: `vile` 在這裡形容天氣或風非常討厭、令人不舒服。
generatedExample: We hurried inside to escape the vile rain.

explanationZhTw: 「make for」表示朝某個方向前進、走向某處。這裡是說溫斯頓朝樓梯走去。
generatedExample: When the meeting ended, she made for the nearest bus stop.

explanationZhTw: 「It is no use doing...」表示做某事沒有用、徒勞無功。
generatedExample: It was no use calling him again because his phone was off.

explanationZhTw: 「cut off」在這裡是指供電被切斷。
generatedExample: The village was cut off after the bridge collapsed.

explanationZhTw: 「economy drive」指節約措施或節省成本的行動。
generatedExample: The office started an economy drive to save paper and electricity.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「contrived」不是單純『設計過』，而是指刻意安排、做作地設計出某種效果。
generatedExample: The scene felt contrived, as if every detail had been staged.

explanationZhTw: 「caption」是圖片下方的標題或說明文字。
generatedExample: The caption explained why the photo was taken.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 fruity 不是「水果味」，而是形容聲音帶點甜膩、圓潤、略高亢的感覺。
generatedExample: A fruity voice announced the train delay over the loudspeaker.

explanationZhTw: read out 指「大聲讀出、念出」，通常是把文字朗讀給別人聽。
generatedExample: The clerk was reading out the winners' names.

explanationZhTw: pig-iron 是「生鐵」，指從礦石初步煉出的粗鐵。
generatedExample: The old factory still produced pig-iron for export.

explanationZhTw: oblong 表示「長方形偏長」；plaque 在這裡是「金屬板、牌面」。
generatedExample: A small oblong metal plaque was fixed beside the door.

explanationZhTw: shut off 表示「關掉、切斷」，這裡是把裝置完全停止運作。
generatedExample: I tried shutting it off, but the alarm kept ringing.

explanationZhTw: smallish 表示「有點小、偏小」，語氣比 small 更不精確。
generatedExample: He lived in a smallish apartment near the station.

explanationZhTw: meagreness 在這裡指「瘦弱、單薄」；也可泛指「不足、稀少」。
generatedExample: The meagreness of the meal left everyone hungry.

explanationZhTw: 窗玻璃片；這裡指窗戶上的一片玻璃。
generatedExample: The window-pane was cracked in the corner.

explanationZhTw: 渦流、小旋渦；這裡指風吹起的小型旋轉氣流。
generatedExample: Small eddies formed behind the rocks.

explanationZhTw: 快速旋轉、打轉；這裡形容風把灰塵和紙屑捲起來。
generatedExample: Leaves were whirling across the road.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「抹上石膏」，而是指海報被大量張貼，到處都是。
generatedExample: The wall was plastered everywhere with election posters.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指特別顯眼、具有支配感的位置。
generatedExample: They placed the statue in a commanding corner of the square.

explanationZhTw: 圖片、海報或插圖旁的標題或說明文字。
generatedExample: The caption explained who took the photo.

explanationZhTw: 斷斷續續地、不規則地。
generatedExample: He slept fitfully through the night.

explanationZhTw: 交替地、輪流地。
generatedExample: The lights flashed alternately red and green.

explanationZhTw: 像掠過表面一樣低空快速移動；這裡指直升機低飛掠過。
generatedExample: The drone skimmed down over the field.

explanationZhTw: 猛然快速離開。
generatedExample: A cat darted away when it heard the noise.

explanationZhTw: 偷偷窺探、刺探；帶有鬼鬼祟祟的語氣。
generatedExample: He was snooping through my desk drawers.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指監視人民思想的秘密警察，不只是監控行為。
generatedExample: In the story, the Thought Police punish people for forbidden ideas.

explanationZhTw: 指生鐵，一種工業用金屬材料；這裡屬於具體名詞詞彙，放在政治宣傳語境中出現。
generatedExample: The factory shipped pig-iron to the steel plant.

explanationZhTw: 指「超額完成」，是偏正式、抽象的名詞用法；在這裡是計畫經濟語境中的術語。
generatedExample: The report praised the overfulfilment of the yearly target.

explanationZhTw: 指《一九八四》裡的特殊監視裝置，結合螢幕與廣播功能，帶有強烈的小說語境含義，不是一般日常用語。
generatedExample: The telescreen never stopped showing the leader’s speeches.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「被接收到、被偵測到」；不是單純的「撿起來」。
generatedExample: The microphone picked up every word he said.

explanationZhTw: 指「視野範圍」；這是固定名詞片語，用來描述人或裝置能看見的範圍。
generatedExample: The camera only covers a small field of vision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「命令」，而是「控制、支配、俯瞰」的意思，指金屬牌控制了可見範圍。
generatedExample: From the hill, the castle commanded the whole valley.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「接上、連線」；在小說裡指秘密監聽或接入某條線路。
generatedExample: The technician plugged in the device and checked the signal.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「無論如何、至少、不管怎樣」；用來轉折並強調後面的說法。
generatedExample: It was late, but at any rate we finished the work.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「被仔細檢查、被嚴密審視」；在這裡強調監控的細緻與壓迫感。
generatedExample: The contract was scrutinized by the legal team.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》裡的雙向監視螢幕／裝置；不是一般電視，而是兼具監看功能的監控媒體。
generatedExample: He spoke quietly because the telescreen might be listening.

explanationZhTw: 這是帶有反諷的專有名詞，指的是負責宣傳與改寫資訊的政府機構，不是真的「真理部」。
generatedExample: The Ministry of Truth rewrote yesterday's news overnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「塔」，而是指建築物高高矗立、壓過周圍環境。
generatedExample: The old library towered above the smaller shops nearby.

explanationZhTw: 形容佈滿黑垢、灰塵或髒污；這裡是在描寫陰沉骯髒的城市景象。
generatedExample: After the storm, the windows were grimy and hard to see through.

explanationZhTw: 指一種模糊、不強烈但明顯存在的厭惡感。
generatedExample: She looked at the meal with vague distaste.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指勉強擠出、逼出某樣東西；這裡是指努力回想記憶。
generatedExample: He squeezed out a few words before falling silent.

explanationZhTw: 原意是「遠景、寬闊的景觀」；這裡指映入眼簾的一大片城市景象。
generatedExample: From the hill, we could see wide vistas of the valley below.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指用支撐物把東西撐住、加固。
generatedExample: Workers shored up the wall before the next storm arrived.

explanationZhTw: 波紋鐵皮，一種表面有凹凸波紋、常用於屋頂或臨時建築的金屬板材。
generatedExample: The shed roof was covered with corrugated iron.

explanationZhTw: 指因重量、老舊或撐不住而下垂、鬆垮。
generatedExample: The shelf was sagging under the weight of the books.

explanationZhTw: 指被炸彈炸毀、留下破壞痕跡的地方。
generatedExample: The bombed sites were still unsafe months later.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「落後、掉隊」的意思，而是指植物零散地蔓延、生長。
generatedExample: Weeds straggled along the edge of the path.

explanationZhTw: 瓦礫、殘骸，通常指建築物被毀後留下的碎石、碎塊。
generatedExample: After the explosion, only rubble remained.

explanationZhTw: 形容骯髒、破敗、令人不快，常帶有道德上不光彩的意味。
generatedExample: They lived in a sordid little room behind the factory.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「沒用、徒勞無功」。
generatedExample: It was no use arguing after the decision had been made.

explanationZhTw: 單數為 tableau，指靜止的場景或畫面；這裡是指零碎、像畫面一樣的記憶片段。
generatedExample: The story ended with a final tableau of the whole family on stage.

explanationZhTw: 難以理解、無法辨認其意義；這裡形容那些記憶片段幾乎看不懂。
generatedExample: Her handwriting was so messy that the note was unintelligible.

explanationZhTw: 《新語》：小說中極權政權使用的官方語言，刻意壓縮詞彙、限制表達與思考。
generatedExample: Newspeak was designed to narrow the range of thought.

explanationZhTw: 出人意料地；非常令人驚訝地。這裡強調它和周圍所有東西都「很不一樣」。
generatedExample: Her comment was startlingly different from the others.

explanationZhTw: 巨大的；尺寸或規模非常大。
generatedExample: They lived in an enormous house on the hill.

explanationZhTw: 金字塔形的；外形像金字塔，往上逐層收窄。
generatedExample: The museum has a pyramidal roof made of glass.

explanationZhTw: 閃閃發亮的；表面反射光線，看起來很亮。
generatedExample: The lake looked glittering in the morning sun.

explanationZhTw: 高高向上延伸；這裡形容建築物往上拔起、越來越高。
generatedExample: The kite was soaring up into the sky.

explanationZhTw: 被清楚地標示出來、凸顯出來；這裡指字樣在白色牆面上很醒目。
generatedExample: The exit sign was picked out in red above the door.

explanationZhTw: 優雅的字體／字樣；lettering 指字的書寫或設計風格，不只是單個字母。
generatedExample: The invitation used elegant lettering in gold ink.

explanationZhTw: （小說中的）黨；特指執政的政治組織，不是一般的社交聚會。
generatedExample: The Party controlled every part of daily life.

explanationZhTw: 這是《一九八四》中的政權口號，表面上把「戰爭」說成「和平」；在這個語境裡，它不是字面意思，而是要表達透過持續戰爭來維持社會控制與穩定的反諷性宣傳語。
generatedExample: In the novel, 'war is peace' is a propaganda slogan, not a literal statement.

explanationZhTw: 這句是反諷式口號，意思不是自由真的等於奴役，而是指在該政權的語境下，個人自由會被描述成會導致混亂、脆弱或被體制消滅的狀態。
generatedExample: The slogan 'freedom is slavery' flips the meaning of freedom to control people.

explanationZhTw: 這句話在此是政治宣傳語，並非一般字面敘述；它要表達的是人民越不了解真相，就越容易被統治，因此「無知」被包裝成政權的「力量」。
generatedExample: According to the regime, ignorance is strength because uninformed people are easier to control.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「真理部」是諷刺性的機構名稱，不是真的追求真相，而是用來操控資訊與宣傳。
generatedExample: The Ministry of Truth sounded noble, but it was actually a propaganda office.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `dwarf` 是動詞，表示「使相形見絀、顯得小很多」，不是名詞「侏儒」。
generatedExample: The new tower dwarfs the old houses beside it.

explanationZhTw: `apparatus` 在這裡指「體系、機構、整套運作架構」，也就是政府的組織系統。
generatedExample: The election changed the political apparatus of the country.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `concerned itself with` 不是「擔心」，而是「負責、處理、管轄」。
generatedExample: Our team concerns itself with customer support and training.

explanationZhTw: `maintained law and order` 是固定說法，意思是「維持治安與社會秩序」。
generatedExample: The police maintained law and order during the protest.

explanationZhTw: `Newspeak` 是小說中的虛構語言，設計目的是縮減可表達的概念，進而控制思想。
generatedExample: In Newspeak, words with politically inconvenient meanings were removed.

explanationZhTw: 形容「令人害怕的、駭人的」。這裡用來強調這棟建築給人的恐怖感受。
generatedExample: The dark corridor looked frightening at night.

explanationZhTw: 指「公務、正式業務」。這裡表示只有經授權、因公務需要才能進入。
generatedExample: She entered the building on official business.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是抽象的「看穿」，而是「穿過、突破」。用在這裡有強烈的強行通過、闖入感。
generatedExample: They kept penetrating through the thick fog.

explanationZhTw: 指「迷宮」，也可比喻複雜、難以通行的路徑或系統。
generatedExample: The office building felt like a maze.

explanationZhTw: 本意是「纏繞物、糾結狀態」，這裡指帶刺鐵絲網等一層層阻礙。
generatedExample: The hikers got stuck in the tree roots and other entanglements.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「通往、一路延伸到」。這裡描述街道一路連到外圍防線。
generatedExample: A long path was leading up to the castle gate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「漫遊」，而是指守衛在附近巡走、到處活動，有警戒和控制的意味。
generatedExample: Security officers roamed the hallway after the alarm rang.

explanationZhTw: 指「警棍、短棍」之類的近身武器，常見於警察或守衛配備。
generatedExample: The police carried truncheons during the protest.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「轉身、回過身來」的意思。
generatedExample: She turned round when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「突然地、唐突地」，強調動作很快。
generatedExample: He stopped abruptly in the middle of the sentence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「把臉部表情調成／固定成」某種樣子，帶有刻意控制表情的意思。
generatedExample: She set her features into a polite smile before answering.

explanationZhTw: 表示「明智的；建議這樣做的」。
generatedExample: It is advisable to book tickets early.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的特定名詞，指一種兼具螢幕與監視功能的裝置；屬於情境特定用語。
generatedExample: The telescreen watched every move in the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「放棄、犧牲掉」的意思，不是宗教上的獻祭。
generatedExample: She sacrificed her free time to finish the report.

explanationZhTw: 表示「知道的；意識到的」。
generatedExample: I was aware that the deadline had changed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一大塊」，尤其是食物的一大塊。
generatedExample: He ate a hunk of bread with the soup.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「必須被留著／得留到以後用」，是義務或必要性的說法。
generatedExample: That money had got to be saved for rent.

explanationZhTw: 表示「散發出（氣味、熱、光等）」。
generatedExample: The machine gave off a loud noise.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「鼓起勇氣、讓自己鎮定下來」的意思。
generatedExample: She nerved herself to ask for help.

explanationZhTw: 表示「大口吞下；狼吞虎嚥地喝下」。
generatedExample: He gulped the water down in one go.

explanationZhTw: 指臉瞬間變得通紅，這裡暗示因痛苦或難堪而臉紅。
generatedExample: He turned scarlet when everyone looked at him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指液體從眼睛流出，也就是眼淚湧出；不是字面上的「用完」。
generatedExample: Tears ran out of her eyes after the wind blew into them.

explanationZhTw: 指身體或心理上的感覺、感受。
generatedExample: I had a strange sensation in my stomach before the exam.

explanationZhTw: 指強烈程度慢慢減弱、平息下來。
generatedExample: The noise died down after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 指不小心地、缺乏謹慎地。
generatedExample: He incautiously left his phone on the bench.

explanationZhTw: 較正式的連接詞，意思是「接著就／於是」。
generatedExample: She opened the box, whereupon everyone cheered.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的虛構裝置，像電視一樣的螢幕，用來播送和監視。
generatedExample: The telescreen flashed with the evening news.

explanationZhTw: 指大小接近四開本，這裡是在描述書本尺寸。
generatedExample: I bought a quarto-sized notebook for sketching.

explanationZhTw: 指《一九八四》裡那種兼具電視與監視功能的裝置；不是一般的「電視」，而是帶有監控意味的特殊名詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen never stopped watching the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 command 不是「命令」，而是「俯視並控制／涵蓋」整個房間的意思，屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: From the hill, the tower seemed to command the whole valley.

explanationZhTw: 指牆邊凹進去的小空間或壁龕；在這裡是房間裡較隱蔽的一角。
generatedExample: She placed the lamp in the alcove beside the door.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「不在……的覆蓋範圍內」；這裡指躲開電視螢幕的視線可及範圍。
generatedExample: We moved farther back to stay outside the range of the camera.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「就視線而言」；意思是只要從看得到的角度來說，他算是躲開了。這是較書面、帶限制條件的表達。
generatedExample: So far as sight went, the object was hidden behind the tree.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 geography 不是「地理」，而是比喻房間的空間格局、配置方式；屬於語境中的特別用法。
generatedExample: The unusual geography of the office made it easy to find a quiet corner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `peculiarly` 不是「古怪地」的負面意思，而是「特別、格外」；用來加強 `beautiful` 的程度。
generatedExample: The garden was peculiarly quiet after the storm.

explanationZhTw: `creamy` 指像奶油一樣的顏色或質感，這裡形容紙張是淡米白、帶暖色調。
generatedExample: She chose a notebook with creamy pages.

explanationZhTw: `manufactured` 是「製造、工業生產」；這裡強調這種紙已經很多年沒有再生產了。
generatedExample: These parts are manufactured in a factory overseas.

explanationZhTw: `frowsy` 形容地方或東西髒亂、邋遢、缺乏整理，這裡是在描寫舊書店的破敗感。
generatedExample: We found the map in a frowsy old shop near the station.

explanationZhTw: `slummy` 形容「貧困、骯髒、破舊」的地區，常帶有貶義。
generatedExample: They moved away from the slummy part of the city.

explanationZhTw: `stricken` 在這裡不是「受傷」或「患病」，而是「突然被強烈情緒擊中、立刻受到影響」的寫法。
generatedExample: I was stricken by guilt the moment I heard the news.

explanationZhTw: `possess` 意思是「擁有、占有」；這裡帶有很強的渴望，想把那本書據為己有。
generatedExample: I wanted to possess the first edition of the novel.

explanationZhTw: `were supposed not to` 表示「按規定不應該」或「被要求不要」；這裡是在說黨員照理不能去一般商店。
generatedExample: We were supposed not to open the package before noon.

explanationZhTw: 這是文中的固定說法，指在官方管制之外買賣東西，也就是「自由市場／黑市式交易」的意思。
generatedExample: People were punished for dealing on the free market.

explanationZhTw: `get hold of` 是片語動詞，意思是「弄到、取得」；這裡指只能透過別的方法才買得到。
generatedExample: I couldn't get hold of her before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `guiltily` 表示「心虛地、帶著罪惡感地」；這裡暗示他覺得自己做了不該做的事。
generatedExample: She looked guiltily at the broken vase.

explanationZhTw: `briefcase` 是公事包、手提公文包，常用來裝文件。
generatedExample: He packed the contract into his briefcase.

explanationZhTw: `compromising` 在這裡不是一般的「妥協的」，而是「可能惹麻煩、足以構成把柄的」；整個片語指這本書本身就很危險、不能被發現。
generatedExample: The letter became a compromising possession after the scandal.

explanationZhTw: illegal：違法的；這裡因為「已經沒有法律」，所以是帶有諷刺意味的用法。
generatedExample: It is illegal to park here after midnight.

explanationZhTw: illegal：違法的；這裡因為「已經沒有法律」，所以是帶有諷刺意味的用法。
generatedExample: It is illegal to park here after midnight.

explanationZhTw: detected：被發現、被察覺到。這裡指秘密行為一旦被發現，就會有嚴重後果。
generatedExample: If the error is detected early, it is easier to fix.

explanationZhTw: punished：處罰、懲罰。這裡是被施加嚴厲刑罰。
generatedExample: The students were punished for cheating on the test.

explanationZhTw: forced-labour camp：強制勞動營／勞改營。這是歷史或政治語境中的嚴重懲罰場所。
generatedExample: The regime sent dissidents to a forced-labour camp.

explanationZhTw: nib：筆尖。這裡指鋼筆或鵝毛筆最前端用來沾墨寫字的部分。
generatedExample: The nib of the pen scratched the paper.

explanationZhTw: penholder：筆桿、筆架；這裡是用來裝筆尖的握柄。
generatedExample: She kept the old fountain pen in a wooden penholder.

explanationZhTw: archaic：古老的、過時的。這裡強調這支筆很舊式、不常用了。
generatedExample: That archaic machine still works, but barely.

explanationZhTw: seldom：很少、難得。比常見的「rarely」更文學一些。
generatedExample: We seldom eat out during the week.

explanationZhTw: procured：設法取得、弄到手。通常暗示經過一番努力或不太容易。
generatedExample: He procured a ticket at the last minute.

explanationZhTw: furtively：偷偷地、鬼鬼祟祟地。這裡表示他買或拿筆的方式很隱秘。
generatedExample: She glanced furtively at her phone during class.

explanationZhTw: scratched：這裡不是「刮傷」，而是指用很粗糙、隨便的方式寫字。
generatedExample: He scratched a note on the back of the envelope.

explanationZhTw: dictate：口述讓別人寫下，或把內容輸入到機器中。這裡是用口說來代替手寫。
generatedExample: She dictated the email to her assistant.

explanationZhTw: speak-write：文中虛構的語音書寫裝置，意思接近「口述輸入／語音轉寫機」。
generatedExample: The office used a speak-write device for quick reports.

explanationZhTw: faltered：猶豫了一下、停頓了一下。這裡表示動作中出現短暫的遲疑。
generatedExample: He faltered before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: tremor：顫抖、震動。這裡指身體內部出現一陣發抖。
generatedExample: A tremor ran through her hands as she spoke.

explanationZhTw: bowels：腸子；在這裡是文學式寫法，表示從身體深處湧起的恐懼或緊張。
generatedExample: Fear seemed to rise from his bowels.

explanationZhTw: decisive：決定性的、關鍵的。這裡表示「在紙上留下第一筆」是最重要的一步。
generatedExample: Her decisive action saved the project.

explanationZhTw: clumsy：笨拙的、不工整的。這裡形容字寫得不漂亮、不熟練。
generatedExample: He wrote the address in clumsy handwriting.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，指「向後靠坐、往後倚」；這裡表示他先放鬆身體、停下動作，帶出無力感。
generatedExample: She sat back and waited for the test results.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，字面是「降臨到……身上」，此處是比喻「無力感忽然襲上心頭」。
generatedExample: A strange silence descended upon the room.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「首先；一開始先說」，常用來引出第一個理由或起點。
generatedExample: To begin with, we need to check the map.

explanationZhTw: 表示「大約、差不多」，用來說明日期或數字不精確。
generatedExample: We arrived round about noon.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「精確確定、弄清楚」；這裡指很難把日期鎖定到更準確的範圍。
generatedExample: We still can't pin down the exact cause of the error.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「突然想到／意識到」，不是字面上的「發生在他身上」。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I had left my keys at home.

explanationZhTw: 指「尚未出生的人／未來世代」，是較文學化、抽象的用法。
generatedExample: They planted trees for the unborn.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「盤旋」的物理動作，而是指思緒短暫停留、徘徊在某件事上。
generatedExample: My mind hovered on the same question all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「突然停住、猛然碰上某個結論或障礙」，帶有意外、突兀的感覺。
generatedExample: The argument fetched up with a bump when nobody could agree.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的自創詞，指同時相信兩個互相矛盾的說法而不覺得衝突。
generatedExample: The report felt like doublethink because it praised the cuts while admitting the damage.

explanationZhTw: 表示某件事終於被他真正體會到、深刻意識到。
generatedExample: The risk came home to her when she saw the accident report.

explanationZhTw: 指「困境、進退兩難的處境」，屬於較正式的詞。
generatedExample: She knew the predicament was serious but still had no clear answer.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示呆呆地、茫然地凝視，不是「愚蠢地看」。
generatedExample: He sat gazing stupidly at the screen after the bad news.

explanationZhTw: 表示切換到、轉成。
generatedExample: The app changed over to night mode automatically.

explanationZhTw: 指刺耳、尖銳、令人不舒服的音樂。
generatedExample: The strident music made the room feel tense.

explanationZhTw: 在此處不是「好奇的」，而是「奇怪的、出人意料的」。
generatedExample: It was curious that the train arrived early for once.

explanationZhTw: 指「準備好」。
generatedExample: I spent the morning making ready for the presentation.

explanationZhTw: 表示「忽然想到；掠過腦海」。
generatedExample: It never crossed my mind that she would leave.

explanationZhTw: 表示把腦中的想法寫下來。
generatedExample: She transferred her notes to paper before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 指冗長到幾乎沒完沒了的獨白。
generatedExample: His speech turned into an interminable monologue.

explanationZhTw: 表示變得無法再繼續，這裡是「突然停住、消失」。
generatedExample: My ideas dried up halfway through the essay.

explanationZhTw: 靜脈曲張形成的潰瘍。
generatedExample: The doctor treated the varicose ulcer carefully.

explanationZhTw: 表示發炎、紅腫惡化。
generatedExample: If the wound becomes inflamed, call a doctor.

explanationZhTw: 表示時間一分一秒地過去。
generatedExample: The minutes were ticking by while we waited.

explanationZhTw: 表示刺耳地、很大聲地響。
generatedExample: The speakers were blaring all night.

explanationZhTw: 指微醺、輕微酒意。
generatedExample: After two drinks, he felt a slight booziness.

explanationZhTw: 「sheer panic」表示「完全驚慌失措、極度恐慌」；這裡是強調恐慌的程度很高。
generatedExample: She forgot her lines in sheer panic and walked off stage.

explanationZhTw: 「setting down」在這裡是「寫下、記錄」的意思，不是單純的「放下」。
generatedExample: Please set down your name and phone number on the form.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「零散地走」；是形容字跡歪歪斜斜、上下擺動，沿著頁面不規則地延伸。
generatedExample: The road straggled up and down the hillside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「shedding」是「丟掉、逐漸失去」的意思，不是「掉落毛髮」或「排出」的常見用法。
generatedExample: The old company logo is slowly shedding its original meaning.

explanationZhTw: 「capital letters」是「大寫字母」；這是基本寫作與拼字相關用語。
generatedExample: Use capital letters at the start of sentences.

explanationZhTw: 「full stops」是英式英文的「句號」；這裡指連標點都沒寫完整。
generatedExample: Remember to add full stops at the end of each sentence.

explanationZhTw: 口語，指「電影院」或「看電影」；這裡是比較隨便的說法，不是正式用語。
generatedExample: We went to the flicks after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「難民」；指因戰爭、迫害或災難而被迫離開家園的人。
generatedExample: The refugees were given food and blankets.

explanationZhTw: 表示「覺得好笑、有趣」；這裡是觀眾因畫面而發笑。
generatedExample: The children were amused by the clown.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般「打滾」那種感覺，而是指在水中笨拙、費力地游動。
generatedExample: The dog was wallowing in the mud.

explanationZhTw: 「鼠海豚」；一種在海裡生活的哺乳動物。
generatedExample: We saw a porpoise near the boat.

explanationZhTw: 槍或武器上的「瞄準器」；這裡指從直升機的瞄準具看過去。
generatedExample: He looked through the gunsights before firing.

explanationZhTw: 「救生艇」；用來在緊急狀況下救人的小船。
generatedExample: A lifeboat rescued everyone from the sinking ship.

explanationZhTw: 表示「盤旋、懸停在上方」；這裡形容直升機停留在空中不動。
generatedExample: The helicopter was hovering above the field.

explanationZhTw: 船的「船頭」；不是弓箭的弓。這裡指救生艇前端的位置。
generatedExample: She stood at the bow and watched the waves.

explanationZhTw: 原意是「鑽洞、挖洞」；這裡是比喻用法，指孩子想把自己整個縮進母親懷裡尋求保護。
generatedExample: The rabbit tried to burrow into the soil.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 planted 不是「種植」，而是「放置、投下」炸彈；屬於軍事語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The attacker planted a bomb near the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「碎木片」；這裡指船被炸得粉碎、成了殘骸。
generatedExample: The explosion left the car in matchwood.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡對「無產者、底層群眾」的簡稱，帶有社會階級意味。
generatedExample: The proles live in the poorer part of the city.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「大吵大鬧、強烈抱怨、製造麻煩」。
generatedExample: He started kicking up a fuss about the delay.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡表示「把她趕出去、驅離」。
generatedExample: Security turned her out after the argument.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡對「無產者、底層群眾」的簡稱，帶有社會階級意味。
generatedExample: The proles live in the poorer part of the city.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡對「無產者、底層群眾」的簡稱，帶有社會階級意味。
generatedExample: The proles live in the poorer part of the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `cramp` 指肌肉抽筋或痙攣引起的疼痛，文中是在說他寫到手部抽筋。
generatedExample: I had to stop running because of a cramp in my calf.

explanationZhTw: `pour out` 這裡不是指真的「倒出來」，而是指把想法或話語大量、急促地說出或寫出來。
generatedExample: She poured out her worries to her sister.

explanationZhTw: `rubbish` 這裡不是字面上的「垃圾」，而是指「胡言亂語、廢話、沒意義的內容」。
generatedExample: What he said was complete rubbish.

explanationZhTw: `clarified itself` 在這裡表示記憶在腦中自己變得清楚；這是比字面用法更偏隱喻的說法。
generatedExample: As we talked, the answer gradually clarified itself in my mind.

explanationZhTw: `felt equal to` 在這裡是「覺得自己有能力／有把握去做某事」的意思，不是字面上的「等於」。
generatedExample: After some rest, I felt equal to finishing the report.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Ministry` 指政府部門或機關。首字母大寫通常表示特定的官方單位，不是一般「事工」的意思。
generatedExample: She worked at the Ministry and handled public records.

explanationZhTw: `nebulous` 在這裡不是字面上的「像雲霧一樣」，而是指事情模糊不清、難以具體描述。
generatedExample: His plan for the future was still nebulous and unfinished.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是特定機構中的「檔案／文書部門」；屬於情境性名詞，用來交代工作場所。
generatedExample: I spent the afternoon in the Records Department sorting archived files.

explanationZhTw: `drag out` 在這裡表示費力地把椅子拉出來；是日常動作的描述。
generatedExample: They were dragging the chairs out of the classroom before the event started.

explanationZhTw: 表示「為了準備……」；常見固定搭配。
generatedExample: We arrived early in preparation for the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 表示只認得外表、見過但不熟；是慣用說法。
generatedExample: I know her by sight, but I don't know her name.

explanationZhTw: 表示「大概、推測上」；這裡是根據前面的線索作出推斷。
generatedExample: Presumably, the train is delayed because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: `spanner` 是「扳手」；屬於工具詞彙。
generatedExample: He tightened the bolt with a spanner.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「思想純潔」，在這裡指保守、講究道德潔淨的態度；屬於帶有意識形態色彩的用法。
generatedExample: The school promoted a kind of clean-mindedness that discouraged any hint of rebellion.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「照單全收口號的人」；不是字面上的吞嚥。
generatedExample: He dismissed them as swallowers of slogans rather than independent thinkers.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「專門找出不正統言行的人」；是帶敵意的創造性說法。
generatedExample: The committee behaved like nosers-out of unorthodoxy, hunting for tiny mistakes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面穿透，而是表示目光彷彿看穿他、直刺人心。
generatedExample: Her stare seemed to pierce right into him and make him nervous.

explanationZhTw: 表示「突然想到、閃過腦海」；是常見慣用說法。
generatedExample: It crossed my mind that I had left the door unlocked.

explanationZhTw: 這是作品中的專有名詞，指監控思想的警察機構；屬於情境性詞彙。
generatedExample: Everyone feared the Thought Police because even private thoughts felt unsafe.

explanationZhTw: 「Inner Party 的成員」；指屬於最高層權力圈的人。
generatedExample: She was admitted as a member of the Inner Party equivalent in the organization.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 remote 不是單指地理上的遙遠，而是指這個職位很高、很難接近。
generatedExample: His office was remote and difficult for ordinary staff to reach.

explanationZhTw: a dim idea = 很模糊、只知道一點點。
generatedExample: I only had a dim idea of how the machine worked.

explanationZhTw: momentary hush = 短暫的靜默，氣氛突然安靜下來。
generatedExample: A momentary hush fell over the room before the announcement.

explanationZhTw: formidable appearance = 外表看起來很嚇人、很有威勢。
generatedExample: The coach had a formidable appearance, but he was kind.

explanationZhTw: resettling 這裡是指重新扶正、調整眼鏡的位置，不是「重新定居」。
generatedExample: He kept resettling his glasses on his nose while he read.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 disarming 不是「解除武裝」，而是「讓人卸下戒心、覺得他很親切」。
generatedExample: Her disarming smile made everyone relax.

explanationZhTw: drawn to someone = 被某人吸引、自然想接近對方。
generatedExample: I was drawn to him because he spoke so calmly.

explanationZhTw: orthodoxy 指「正統、主流且被要求遵守的觀念」；這裡是政治上的標準立場。
generatedExample: The article challenged the political orthodoxy of the time.

explanationZhTw: written in his face = 他的表情或神情很明顯地透露出來。
generatedExample: Worry was written in her face after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: at any rate = 不管怎樣、無論如何，用來轉回重點或降低前一句的確定性。

explanationZhTw: stay in = 留在室內或待在原地，不出去。
generatedExample: He decided to stay in until the rain stopped.

explanationZhTw: cubicle = 小隔間、工作站裡的小空間。
generatedExample: My desk is in a small cubicle near the window.

explanationZhTw: 「hideous」表示極度令人厭惡、可怕或醜陋。這裡是在強調聲音很刺耳、令人不舒服。
generatedExample: The room was filled with a hideous smell after the fire.

explanationZhTw: 「grinding screech」指尖銳刺耳、像摩擦般刮人的尖叫聲。
generatedExample: We heard a grinding screech when the train brakes were applied.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是《一九八四》中的虛構裝置，兼具螢幕、廣播與監控功能。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the corner watched every move the family made.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示聲音、氣味或感覺讓人極度不舒服、聽了渾身不對勁。
generatedExample: That high-pitched alarm sound really set my teeth on edge.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是形象化說法，表示毛骨悚然、背脊發麻，強調強烈的不安感。
generatedExample: The strange footsteps in the empty hall bristled the hair at the back of my neck.

explanationZhTw: 對某人貼上「人民公敵」的政治標籤；在此是宣傳式稱呼。
generatedExample: The newspaper called the activist the Enemy of the People.

explanationZhTw: hisses 指眾人發出的嘶聲或噓聲，用來表達不滿或厭惡。
generatedExample: A wave of hisses came from the back row.

explanationZhTw: squeak 這裡不是動詞，而是名詞，指一聲尖細短促的叫聲。
generatedExample: She gave a squeak when the door slammed.

explanationZhTw: renegade 指背叛原立場、陣營或信仰的人。
generatedExample: After the scandal, the renegade minister quit the party.

explanationZhTw: backslider 原指信仰倒退者，這裡也是「倒戈者／背離者」。
generatedExample: He was a backslider who returned to his bad habits.

explanationZhTw: counter-revolutionary activities 指反革命行動，是政治語境中的正式說法。
generatedExample: The rebels were accused of counter-revolutionary activities.

explanationZhTw: condemned to death 指被判處死刑。
generatedExample: The spy was condemned to death after the trial.

explanationZhTw: Two Minutes Hate 是《1984》裡的專有名詞，指被強迫集體仇恨的宣傳活動。
generatedExample: Everyone had to watch the Two Minutes Hate before work.

explanationZhTw: primal traitor 在這裡是誇張的政治用語，意思是「最早、最根本的叛徒」。
generatedExample: In the speech, he was painted as the primal traitor.

explanationZhTw: sprang directly out of 表示「直接源自」，語氣像固定片語。
generatedExample: His anger sprang directly out of the bad news.

explanationZhTw: hatching his conspiracies 指「醞釀／策劃陰謀」，hatch 這裡是比喻用法。
generatedExample: Police said the suspect was hatching his conspiracies in secret.

explanationZhTw: beyond the sea 在這裡不是單純指海的另一邊，而是「海外／遠方」的含意。
generatedExample: They said the exiled prince was living beyond the sea.

explanationZhTw: foreign paymasters 指「境外金主／出錢的外部支持者」，帶有貶義。
generatedExample: The regime survived thanks to foreign paymasters.

explanationZhTw: 「constricted」在這裡不是一般的「狹窄」，而是指橫膈膜因緊張、壓力或恐懼而發緊、受壓，帶出身體上的不適感。
generatedExample: My chest felt constricted before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 「despicable」表示「可鄙的、卑劣的」，用來強烈表達對某人的道德厭惡。
generatedExample: He was acting in a despicable way toward his teammates.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「perverse」不是單純的「反常」，而是指刻意扭曲、故意唱反調的說法，帶有挑釁和歪曲事實的意味。
generatedExample: Her perverse comments made the discussion harder to follow.

explanationZhTw: 「see through it」是片語動詞，意思是「看穿、識破」，尤其是看穿謊言、騙局或不誠實的說法。
generatedExample: I could see through his excuse immediately.

explanationZhTw: 「level-headed」表示「冷靜理智的、頭腦清楚的」，這裡用來對比那些可能會被騙的人。
generatedExample: She stayed level-headed during the emergency.

explanationZhTw: 「taken in」是片語動詞，意思是「被騙、被蒙蔽」，常用於某人相信了不真實的說法。
generatedExample: Many people were taken in by the fake message.

explanationZhTw: 「advocating」意思是「主張、提倡」，語氣比一般的「support」更正式，常用於理念、權利或政策。
generatedExample: The speaker was advocating better access to education.

explanationZhTw: 「parody」在這裡是「戲仿、模仿性的諷刺作品」，不是單純的「模仿」，而是刻意用相似風格來取笑或批評。
generatedExample: The skit was a parody of political speeches.

explanationZhTw: 「specious claptrap」是帶強烈貶義的說法，意思接近「貌似有理但其實空洞的廢話／騙人話術」；這裡強調宣傳語言外表像真的，實際上很空洞。
generatedExample: His speech was specious claptrap designed to distract people.

explanationZhTw: 「bleating」字面是羊叫聲，這裡用來形容 Goldstein 的聲音像羊一樣尖弱、惱人，帶有貶抑和嘲諷效果。
generatedExample: His bleating voice made the complaint sound even weaker.

explanationZhTw: 「爆發、突然湧現」；這裡指憤怒情緒突然在群眾中蔓延開來，對應片語 `break out` 的「突然發生」用法。
generatedExample: Panic was breaking out in the station after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 「太過沉重而難以承受」；`borne` 是 `bear` 的被動/過去分詞，這裡不是「出生」而是「忍受、承受」。
generatedExample: The grief was too much to be borne alone.

explanationZhTw: 「仇恨的對象」；這裡的 `object` 不是「物體」，而是「目標、對象」。
generatedExample: The politician quickly became an object of hatred after the scandal.

explanationZhTw: 「被駁斥、被證明錯誤」；`refute` 指用論據或證據否定某種說法。
generatedExample: Her claim was refuted by the data.

explanationZhTw: 「新一批容易受騙的人」；`dupes` 指受騙者、容易被操弄的人。
generatedExample: Scammers always look for fresh dupes online.

explanationZhTw: 「被他引誘／迷惑」；這裡不是單純的情色用法，而是指被他的話術或魅力拉攏。
generatedExample: Many voters were seduced by his promises.

explanationZhTw: 「被揭穿、被查出」；字面是「摘下面具」，文中指秘密身分或陰謀被曝光。
generatedExample: The spy was unmasked after years of pretending to be a diplomat.

explanationZhTw: 「神祕、可疑的軍隊」；`shadowy` 這裡不是單純「有陰影的」，而是「隱密、難以捉摸」。
generatedExample: Rumors spread about a shadowy army operating beyond the border.

explanationZhTw: 「推翻政權／國家體制」；`overthrow` 指使現有政府、權力或制度垮台。
generatedExample: They were accused of plotting the overthrow of the government.

explanationZhTw: 「所有異端邪說的彙編」；`compendium` 是彙編、概要，`heresies` 是異端觀點或異端邪說。
generatedExample: The manual is a compendium of all the essential safety rules.

explanationZhTw: 「秘密流傳」；`clandestinely` 表示暗中地、不公開地。
generatedExample: The leaflet circulated clandestinely among the workers.

explanationZhTw: 「模糊不清的傳聞」；`vague` 表示內容不明確、細節不足。
generatedExample: We only heard vague rumours about the merger.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指情緒和氣氛迅速升到狂熱、失控的程度，不是字面上的「上升」。
generatedExample: The crowd rose to a frenzy when the winner was announced.

explanationZhTw: 表示用最大音量、扯開嗓門地喊叫。
generatedExample: They shouted at the tops of their voices so the coach could hear them.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「淹死」，而是指用更大的聲音把別的聲音蓋過去。
generatedExample: We turned up the music to drown the noise outside.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻，指嘴巴一開一合、像離水後掙扎的魚。
generatedExample: He stared at the test paper, opening and closing his mouth like a landed fish.

explanationZhTw: obliged to 表示「不得不」；act a part 表示「演出/裝出某種樣子」。
generatedExample: She felt obliged to act a part at the formal dinner.

explanationZhTw: pretence 指假裝、做作；這裡是說連裝樣子都不需要了。
generatedExample: After a few minutes, the pretence of calm disappeared.

explanationZhTw: ecstasy 在這裡不是「快樂」，而是指一種強烈到近乎失控的亢奮狀態。
generatedExample: The music created a strange ecstasy in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: vindictiveness 指報復心、惡意，想傷害別人來出氣。
generatedExample: His vindictiveness made it hard to forgive him.

explanationZhTw: grimacing 指做出扭曲、痛苦或憤怒的表情。
generatedExample: The child was grimacing because the medicine tasted terrible.

explanationZhTw: 表示對某人產生強烈同情或憐憫。
generatedExample: My heart went out to the family after hearing their story.

explanationZhTw: heretic 指異端者；這裡是被主流權力視為「不合正統」的人。
generatedExample: In the story, the heretic is punished for speaking the truth.

explanationZhTw: 表示與周圍的人想法一致、站在同一邊。
generatedExample: For a moment I felt at one with everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: tower up 表示高高聳立；這裡強調形象突然變得巨大、壓倒性。
generatedExample: The monument seemed to tower up above the city.

explanationZhTw: hordes 指一大群人，常帶有混亂、威脅或貶義。
generatedExample: Hordes of tourists filled the square during the festival.

explanationZhTw: wrecking 這裡是指毀壞、摧毀，不只是「弄壞」而已。
generatedExample: One false rumor can wreck the trust of a whole team.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `switch` 不是單純「切換」，而是指刻意把憎恨轉向別的人或事。
generatedExample: She could switch her attention from worry to work in a second.

explanationZhTw: 幻覺；指腦中出現的虛假影像或感受。
generatedExample: The fever caused hallucinations for several hours.

explanationZhTw: 突然在腦中閃現；這裡是某個想法或畫面迅速出現在腦海中。
generatedExample: A warning flashed through my mind at the last second.

explanationZhTw: 鞭打、狠打；這裡是非常暴力的「用鞭子或棍子抽打」。
generatedExample: The guards flogged the prisoners as punishment.

explanationZhTw: 警棍；警察或保安使用的短棍。
generatedExample: The officer held a truncheon in his right hand.

explanationZhTw: 木樁；這裡指把人綁上去的固定木柱。
generatedExample: They drove a stake into the ground to mark the boundary.

explanationZhTw: 此處是古舊、文學性的用法，指強暴；不是現代較少見的「使陶醉」。
generatedExample: The novel uses an old verb to suggest violent sexual assault.

explanationZhTw: 此處指性高潮，不是一般的「最高潮、頂點」。
generatedExample: The scene ends at the moment of climax.

explanationZhTw: 委婉說法，指「與某人發生性關係」，不是字面上的「去睡覺」。
generatedExample: He realized he did not want to go to bed with her after all.

explanationZhTw: 柔軟而有彈性；也可形容身體線條靈活柔和。
generatedExample: The dancer's supple movements impressed everyone.

explanationZhTw: 令人厭惡的；強烈表達反感或憎惡。
generatedExample: The odious remark made everyone uncomfortable.

explanationZhTw: 貞潔；特別指與性行為克制、沒有性經驗相關的概念。
generatedExample: In the story, the ring symbolizes chastity and restraint.

explanationZhTw: climax 指「高潮、頂點」；這裡是情緒或氣氛達到最強烈的時刻。
generatedExample: The tension in the room rose to its climax before the announcement.

explanationZhTw: bleat 是「羊叫聲」；在這裡用來比喻聲音尖細、刺耳，帶有嘲弄意味。
generatedExample: We heard a sheep's bleat from the field behind the house.

explanationZhTw: flinch 指「因害怕、疼痛或驚嚇而退縮」；這裡是本能地往後一縮。
generatedExample: He flinched backwards when the balloon suddenly popped.

explanationZhTw: deep sigh of relief 是慣用說法，表示「鬆了一大口氣」，強調緊張解除後的放鬆。
generatedExample: When the test was over, she let out a deep sigh of relief.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「融化」，而是指影像或形象迅速變成另一個樣子。
generatedExample: The dark shape on the wall melted into a human figure.

explanationZhTw: uttered 是較正式的「說出、發出」；常用在書面語，表示把話講出口。
generatedExample: She uttered a short warning before closing the door.

explanationZhTw: din 指「連續而刺耳的喧鬧聲」；這裡是戰場上的震耳噪音。
generatedExample: We could barely hear each other over the din of traffic.

explanationZhTw: stood out 是片語動詞，表示「顯眼、突出」；這裡指標語被清楚地呈現出來。
generatedExample: Her bright jacket stood out in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 在這句口號裡，`war` 不是單純指戰爭事件，而是被政權拿來當作控制與統治的正面口號，語意帶有諷刺性的顛倒效果。
generatedExample: The slogan uses war as a tool of control, not as a normal military conflict.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `peace` 不是一般的和平成熟意義，而是被宣稱成由戰爭製造出的表面秩序，屬於刻意顛倒的政治語境。
generatedExample: The regime claims that constant surveillance creates peace.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `freedom` 被重新定義成一種危險或混亂的狀態，和一般「自由」的正面意思相反，是口號式的扭曲用法。
generatedExample: In their propaganda, freedom is described as a threat.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `slavery` 不是字面上的奴役制度說明，而是被拿來代表所謂「真正安全」的服從狀態，屬於語意顛倒。
generatedExample: They told citizens that obedience was a kind of slavery-free life.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `ignorance` 不是一般「無知」的中性描述，而是被包裝成有利於統治的狀態，和常識中的負面評價相反。
generatedExample: The group treated ignorance as something that kept people easy to control.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `strength` 不是指真正的身體或心理力量，而是指政權宣稱由無知帶來的穩定與權力，屬於反常語義。
generatedExample: According to the slogan, not asking questions was a source of strength.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「堅持」，而是指影像「持續停留、久久不散」。
generatedExample: The memory of the accident persisted for years.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語動詞表示效果、感覺或影響「逐漸消退、慢慢減弱」。
generatedExample: The pain started to wear off after an hour.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示她「猛地向前撲去」或「突然整個身體往前一衝」。
generatedExample: He flung himself forward to catch the ball.

explanationZhTw: 形容聲音「微微顫抖、發抖而低弱」，帶有緊張或激動的感覺。
generatedExample: She answered in a tremulous voice.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，指「把臉埋進雙手裡」，常表示難過、羞愧、無法面對。
generatedExample: After hearing the news, he buried his face in his hands.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「明顯的、顯而易見的」，強調作者可以直接看出來。
generatedExample: It was apparent that the meeting had been cancelled.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是較正式的說法，表示「說出、喃喃講出」；比一般的 say 更書面。
generatedExample: He was uttering words I could not understand.

explanationZhTw: 「broke into」在這裡表示「突然開始做某事」，常用來描述一群人一下子開始唱、笑或鼓掌。
generatedExample: The audience broke into applause when the band walked on stage.

explanationZhTw: 「chant」是「有節奏地反覆喊唱」，這裡指群眾整齊、重複地發出聲音。
generatedExample: The students began a chant to support their team.

explanationZhTw: 「refrain」在這裡不是「克制」，而是「反覆出現的歌句或口號」，也就是重複出現的短句。
generatedExample: The song's refrain stayed in my head all day.

explanationZhTw: 「overwhelming」表示「強烈到難以承受的」，用來形容情緒太強烈、壓倒性。
generatedExample: She felt overwhelming emotion when she saw her old school again.

explanationZhTw: 「self-hypnosis」是「自我催眠」，指藉由重複動作或聲音讓自己進入近乎恍神、麻木的狀態。
generatedExample: He used self-hypnosis to calm himself before the exam.

explanationZhTw: 「delirium」在這裡不是單純的發燒胡言亂語，而是指一種失控、狂熱、近乎瘋狂的集體情緒。
generatedExample: The crowd seemed caught in delirium after the final goal.

explanationZhTw: 「dissemble」是「掩飾、隱藏真實感受」，通常用在不讓別人看出自己的想法或情緒。
generatedExample: He tried to dissemble his disappointment, but it was obvious.

explanationZhTw: 「betrayed him」在這裡是「洩露了他的真實想法／出賣了他」，不是字面上的背叛，而是無意間暴露。
generatedExample: A nervous smile betrayed her before she could answer.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是慣用說法，表示「引起對方注意」或「和對方目光相接」。不是字面上的「抓到眼睛」。
generatedExample: Her bright red hat caught my eye across the street.

explanationZhTw: 表示「正在做某事的當下／正要做某事」。這裡是在描述他正把眼鏡重新戴回鼻樑上。
generatedExample: I caught him in the act of deleting the file.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「訊息經過」，而是指「意思已經傳達／彼此已心領神會」。
generatedExample: A quick glance was enough and the message had passed between them.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「輕蔑、鄙視」。語氣比一般的「不喜歡」強很多。
generatedExample: He spoke with contempt about the corrupt official.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「厭惡、反感」。這裡和前面的 contempt、hatred 並列，語氣很強。
generatedExample: The smell of the garbage filled her with disgust.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「支持你、站在你這邊」。
generatedExample: Whatever happens, I'm on your side.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一閃而過的理解或聰明神情」，不是一般的「智慧」本身。
generatedExample: There was a flash of intelligence in his eyes before he looked away.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，表示「高深莫測的、難以看透的」。
generatedExample: Her expression stayed inscrutable throughout the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sequel` 不是電影或小說的「續集」，而是指某件事的後續結果、延伸影響。
generatedExample: The argument had no sequel, so everyone simply moved on.

explanationZhTw: `rumours` 指未經證實、口耳相傳的消息或傳聞。
generatedExample: Rumours spread quickly through the office after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `underground` 這裡不是字面上的「地下」，而是指秘密進行、暗中組織的。
generatedExample: Police uncovered underground conspiracies among the rebels.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `myth` 不是「神話故事」，而是指根本不存在、只是虛構的東西。
generatedExample: For years, the secret tunnel was dismissed as a myth.

explanationZhTw: `fleeting` 表示短暫得幾乎一閃而過；`glimpses` 是短暫的一瞥。合在一起表示非常零碎、轉瞬即逝的線索。
generatedExample: He caught only fleeting glimpses of the suspect in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: `snatches` 在這裡指零碎片段、片段式內容，不是「抓取動作」本身。
generatedExample: We heard snatches of their conversation from the next room.

explanationZhTw: `scribbles` 指潦草亂寫、草草塗鴉的字跡。
generatedExample: He left scribbles in the margin of the notebook.

explanationZhTw: `lavatory` 是較正式或英式的說法，意思是廁所、洗手間。
generatedExample: The lavatory was at the end of the corridor.

explanationZhTw: `cubicle` 指小隔間或狹小的工作空間，這裡是辦公隔間。
generatedExample: She worked alone in a tiny cubicle near the window.

explanationZhTw: `following up` 是片語動詞，意思是繼續追查、後續處理或接著採取行動。
generatedExample: I will follow up our call with an email tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: `crossed his mind` 是慣用說法，表示某個念頭突然掠過腦海、想到一下。
generatedExample: It never crossed my mind that the plan could fail.

explanationZhTw: `set about` 是片語動詞，表示開始著手做某件事。
generatedExample: We need to set about fixing the problem right away.

explanationZhTw: `equivocal` 表示模稜兩可、不明確，讓人無法確定真正意思。
generatedExample: Her answer was equivocal, so I still did not know what she wanted.

explanationZhTw: `locked loneliness` 是帶有隱喻的說法，指一種被困住、無法逃離的封閉孤獨感。
generatedExample: After moving abroad, he felt the locked loneliness of living in a new city alone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「roused himself」是指他讓自己清醒、振作起來，不是字面上的「叫醒別人」；語氣帶有勉強恢復精神的感覺。
generatedExample: I roused myself and started the report after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「let out」在這裡表示「發出」，常用來說聲音、氣息或動作被釋放出來。
generatedExample: She let out a sigh when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 「belch」是「打嗝、打出嗝聲」，屬於較常見的日常用詞。
generatedExample: He tried not to belch at the table.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是很具體的身體感受，指酒精或反胃感好像從胃裡往上湧，帶有不舒服、想吐的意思。
generatedExample: After the ride, a sick feeling was rising from my stomach.

explanationZhTw: re-focused 表示「重新對焦、重新把注意力集中」。這裡是說他的視線再次落回頁面上並看清楚了。
generatedExample: Her attention re-focused on the speech after the interruption.

explanationZhTw: musing 是「沉思、出神地想事情」；helplessly 表示「不由自主地」。整個片語寫的是他在發呆思索時，手卻還在自動書寫。
generatedExample: She sat helplessly musing and missed the announcement.

explanationZhTw: automatic action 指「自動化的動作、無意識完成的動作」，不是字面上機器在操作，而是人在沒有刻意思考下做出的舉動。
generatedExample: After years of practice, typing became an automatic action for him.

explanationZhTw: cramped 在這裡形容字跡「擠縮、空間很小」；awkward handwriting 則是「不順眼、不俐落的字跡」。整體是在說字變得比以前更工整。
generatedExample: My cramped, awkward handwriting improved after I slowed down.

explanationZhTw: voluptuously 原意偏向「帶有感官享受、甚至性感地」。這裡是比喻用法，強調筆在紙上滑動得非常順、非常有快感。
generatedExample: The cat stretched voluptuously in the warm sunlight.

explanationZhTw: printing 在這裡不是「列印」，而是「用印刷體書寫、把字寫得像印刷字一樣」。
generatedExample: Please print your name clearly on the form.

explanationZhTw: capitals 指「大寫字母」。large neat capitals 就是「又大又工整的大寫印刷字」。
generatedExample: Write the heading in large neat capitals.

explanationZhTw: 表示「一再地、反覆地」；用來強調某個動作或情況重複很多次。
generatedExample: She checked the message over and over again before sending it.

explanationZhTw: 「twinge of panic」指突然冒出的一陣恐慌感；「twinge」原本有「一陣刺痛、短暫不舒服」的意思，這裡是比喻用法，表示短暫但明顯的不安。
generatedExample: She felt a twinge of panic when she could not find her phone.

explanationZhTw: 「absurd」表示荒謬、不合理；在這裡是指他的反應或想法其實沒有道理，只是情緒上覺得很誇張。
generatedExample: It seemed absurd to worry about such a small mistake.

explanationZhTw: 「tempted」表示「很想做某事、被引誘想做某事」；這裡是指他一度很想把那些頁面撕掉。
generatedExample: I was tempted to skip dinner after such a long meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「tear out」是片語動詞，意思是把東西撕下、撕掉；這裡指把日記裡受損的頁面撕掉。
generatedExample: He tore out the page and threw it away.

explanationZhTw: 「spoiled」在這裡不是「被寵壞的」，而是指「弄髒、毀損、變壞」的頁面。這是語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The rain left the pages spoiled and hard to read.

explanationZhTw: 「abandon」是放棄、停止做；「enterprise」在這裡不是商業公司，而是指這個整體計畫或行動。整句表示他想乾脆完全放棄這件事。
generatedExample: After three failed attempts, they decided to abandon the enterprise altogether.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「沒有用、徒勞無功」；做了也不會改變結果。
generatedExample: It would be useless to argue with him tonight.

explanationZhTw: `refrain from + V-ing` 表示「克制自己不去做某事、忍住不做」。
generatedExample: She refrained from commenting during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「沒有差別、無關緊要」，強調結果一樣。
generatedExample: It made no difference which route we took.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「沒有差別、無關緊要」，強調結果一樣。
generatedExample: His answer made no difference to the final decision.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，字面是「把筆放到紙上」，實際指「開始寫、動筆寫作」。
generatedExample: I finally set pen to paper and wrote the first draft.

explanationZhTw: 這是作者自造的概念，指「連思想本身都被當成犯罪」；不是一般字典詞義。
generatedExample: In the novel, even private doubts could count as Thoughtcrime.

explanationZhTw: `be bound to` 表示「一定會、註定會」；`get you` 在這裡是「抓到你、逮到你」。
generatedExample: If you keep making the same mistake, it is bound to get you eventually.

explanationZhTw: 表示「總是、幾乎不例外」，語氣比 `always` 更正式，也更強調規律性。
generatedExample: The bus invariably arrives late on Mondays.

explanationZhTw: 表示「絕大多數情況」，常見於正式、書面語，語氣比 `most cases` 更強。
generatedExample: In the vast majority of cases, the software works without any changes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「不見了」，而是指人被秘密帶走、失蹤，帶有政治壓迫的語境。
generatedExample: After the coup, several journalists disappeared.

explanationZhTw: 表示從官方名冊中刪除，暗示一個人連存在紀錄都被抹去。
generatedExample: After the fraud case, his name was removed from the registers.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，表示「徹底抹除、銷毀」，這裡指紀錄被完全清除。
generatedExample: A power surge wiped out the data on the server.

explanationZhTw: 表示「廢除、取消」，常用於制度、法律或規則；這裡故意用在人身上，形成冷酷的語感。
generatedExample: The government abolished the old tax rule last year.

explanationZhTw: 表示「徹底毀滅、消滅」，語氣很強，通常比 `destroyed` 更極端。
generatedExample: The fire annihilated the small wooden shed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是特別的語境用法，不是字面上的「汽化」，而是指一個人被從社會與記錄中徹底抹除。
generatedExample: In the story, the dissident was effectively vaporized from public records.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `seized by` 不是「抓住」的字面意思，而是指「突然被……所控制／侵襲」，帶有情緒失控的感覺。
generatedExample: She was seized by panic when she heard the alarm.

explanationZhTw: `hysteria` 指「歇斯底里、情緒失控的狀態」，在文學語境中常用來描述強烈、近乎失控的情緒反應。
generatedExample: The crowd fell into hysteria after the unexpected announcement.

explanationZhTw: `scrawl` 指「潦草的字跡」或「潦草地寫」，這裡描述寫得很急、字跡很亂。
generatedExample: I could barely read his scrawl on the note.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動詞，意思是「開槍射擊」；在這種語境下也暗示「槍決、處決」。
generatedExample: They threatened to shoot anyone who tried to escape.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動詞，意思是「開槍射擊」；在這種語境下也暗示「槍決、處決」。

explanationZhTw: 這是口號式說法，意思是「打倒……、反對……」。
generatedExample: Down with the new tax policy, the protesters shouted.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》裡，這不是單純的「哥哥」，而是指極權政權的象徵人物，代表監控與控制。
generatedExample: In the novel, Big Brother watches every citizen.

explanationZhTw: 「sat back」在這裡是「往後靠坐、向椅背靠去」，表示身體放鬆或稍微退回。
generatedExample: She sat back and listened quietly.

explanationZhTw: 「ashamed」是「感到羞愧、慚愧」，表示對自己的行為或想法覺得不好意思。
generatedExample: He felt ashamed after speaking rudely to his sister.

explanationZhTw: 「laid down」在這裡是「把……放下」，不是字面上的「躺下」。
generatedExample: She laid down the suitcase and opened the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「暴力地開始」，而是指「突然嚇了一跳、猛地一震」。
generatedExample: When the glass fell, he started violently.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是名詞，意思是「敲門聲」或「敲擊聲」。
generatedExample: We heard knocking at the window.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「一動也不動、非常安靜」，像老鼠一樣不發出聲音。
generatedExample: She sat as still as a mouse while the teacher checked the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 futile 表示「徒勞的、沒有可能成功的」；整個片語就是「毫無希望的盼望」。
generatedExample: It was a futile hope to finish the report before lunch.

explanationZhTw: 這裡形容心跳非常快、非常重，像鼓一樣咚咚作響。
generatedExample: My heart was thumping like a drum before the exam started.

explanationZhTw: heavily 這裡不是「重量很大」，而是指動作遲緩、吃力、沉重。
generatedExample: After the long hike, he moved heavily toward the car.
